records.ety: [OE. dame mistress, lady; also, mother, dam. See Dame.]

defs: A female parent; -- used of beasts, especially of quadrupeds; sometimes applied in contempt to a human mother.
quotes: Our sire and dam, now confined to horses, are a relic of this age (13th century) . . . .Dame is used of a hen; we now make a great difference between dame and dam.
The dam runs lowing up and down, Looking the way her harmless young one went.

defs: A king or crowned piece in the game of draughts.

records.ety: [Akin to OLG., D., & Dan. dam, G. & Sw. damm, Icel. dammr, and AS. fordemman to stop up, Goth. Fardammjan.]

defs: A barrier to prevent the flow of a liquid; esp., a bank of earth, or wall of any kind, as of masonry or wood, built across a water course, to confine and keep back flowing water.
A firebrick wall, or a stone, which forms the front of the hearth of a blast furnace.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Dammed (dmd); p. pr. & vb. n. Damming.]

defs: To obstruct or restrain the flow of, by a dam; to confine by constructing a dam, as a stream of water; -- generally used with in or up.
quotes: I'll have the current in this place dammed up.
A weight of earth that dams in the water.

defs: To shut up; to stop up; to close; to restrain.
quotes: The strait pass was dammed With dead men hurt behind, and cowards.
